name: Macadamia Fudge Torte
recipeIngredient: 1 package (1 lb 2.25 oz) pudding-included devil's food cake mix
1 (16 oz) can sliced pears in light syrup, drained
1/3 cup chopped macadamia nuts or pecans
1 (17 oz) jar butterscotch caramel fudge ice cream topping
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Spray a 9- or 10-inch springform pan with non-stick cooking spray.
3. In a small saucepan, combine filling ingredients. Cook over medium-low heat until chocolate is melted, stirring occasionally.
4. In a large bowl, combine cake mix, cinnamon, and oil; blend at low speed for 20 to 30 seconds or until crumbly (the mixture will be dry).
5. Place pears in the blender container or food processor bowl with metal blade; cover and blend until smooth.
6. In a large bowl, combine 2 1/2 cups of the cake mix mixture, pureed pears, and eggs; beat at low speed until moistened.
7. Beat for 2 minutes at medium speed.
8. Spread batter evenly in the spray-coated pan.
9. Drop filling by spoonfuls over the batter.
10. Stir nuts and water into the remaining cake mix mixture. Sprinkle over the filling.
11. Bake at 350°F for 45 to 50 minutes or until the top springs back when touched lightly in the center.
12. Cool for 10 minutes, then remove the sides of the pan.
13. Cool for an additional 1 1/2 hours or until completely cooled.
14. In a small saucepan, combine sauce ingredients. Cook over medium-low heat for 3 to 4 minutes or until well blended, stirring occasionally.
15. To serve, spoon 2 tablespoons warm sauce onto each serving plate; top with a wedge of torte. If desired, serve with vanilla ice cream or frozen yogurt and garnish with chocolate curls.
